query: Locate datasheet- or paper-grade evidence for: (a) acoustic, piezo, or capacitive *dry-powder* microdispensers, including EDC Biosystems ATS Gen5/Gen6 acoustic dispensers used for solid particles, Scienion sciDROP-PICO solid handling options, Labcyte/Beckman Echo dry-powder evaluations, and any academic prototypes for nanoparticle / microparticle acoustic ejection; (b) the specific Mettler Toledo Quantos QX1 and QX2 modules — minimum dose, accuracy, throughput, head types, balance compatibility (XPR/XPE), and integration options; (c) Chemspeed 'Powdernium' branding and the GDU family (GDU-V, GDU-Pfd, GDU-S/SWILE, GDU-P) — minimum dose, accuracy at sub-mg / mg / 10 mg / 100 mg targets, container volumes, supported powder cohesiveness, and dispense-time benchmarks. Be explicit where information is not retrievable.
answer:
## Scope and retrieval status
This response attempted to locate *datasheet-grade or paper-grade* evidence for (a) dry-powder microdispensing by acoustic/piezo/capacitive technologies (specifically EDC ATS Gen5/Gen6 for solid particles, Scienion sciDROP-PICO solid handling, Labcyte/Beckman Echo dry-powder evaluations, and academic particle acoustic ejection), (b) Mettler Toledo Quantos QX1/QX2 module specifications, and (c) Chemspeed “Powdernium” branding and the GDU family specifications.

Across extensive tool-based Google Scholar and patent searches and full-text evidence extraction, *vendor-datasheet* specifications for Quantos QX1/QX2 and Chemspeed Powdernium/GDU variants were **not retrievable in the accessible corpus**, and *dry-powder acoustic microdispensing* evidence for Echo/ATS/sciDROP-PICO was likewise **not retrievable** (see consolidated table below). (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 5-7)

## Consolidated evidence table and explicit gaps
| Item/device | Evidence found (yes/no + what) | Key quantitative specs retrievable (dose min/range, accuracy/precision, throughput/dispense time, container formats) | Integration/compatibility notes | What is explicitly NOT retrievable from current sources |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| EDC Biosystems ATS Gen5 / Gen6 acoustic transfer systems for **dry powder / solid particles** | **No direct dry-powder evidence retrieved.** Accessible papers show ATS/Gen5 used as an acoustic dispenser for dissolved compounds/inhibitors, i.e. liquid dosing workflows rather than dry powder. No retrieved full text demonstrated solid-particle or dry-powder transfer by ATS Gen5/Gen6. | Dry-powder minimum dose/range: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. Accuracy/precision for dry powder: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. Throughput for dry powder: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. Container/source format for dry powder: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | ATS is evidenced only as part of assay/liquid compound dispensing workflows in accessible papers; no solid-handling compatibility details were retrievable. | Whether Gen5/Gen6 support solid particles or dry powders; particle size limits; powder head/cartridge design; plate compatibility for solids; any vendor datasheet numbers; any dry-powder evaluations: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Labcyte / Beckman Echo **dry-powder** evaluations | **No direct dry-powder evidence retrieved.** Retrieved Echo literature is standard ADE for liquids/reagents and does not substantiate dry-powder or free-solid dispensing. | Dry-powder dose specs: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. Dry-powder accuracy/CV: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. Dry-powder throughput: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Retrieved Echo literature supports liquid ADE only; no solid-handling option was evidenced. | Any Echo dry-powder validation study, solid-particle source plate, or datasheet/paper on dry-powder dispensing: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Scienion sciDROP-PICO **solid handling / powder** | **No evidence retrieved** for sciDROP-PICO powder or solid handling in accessible corpus. | Minimum dose/range, accuracy, throughput, particle-size limits, source-cartridge/container details: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | No integration or compatibility evidence retrieved for solids/powders. | Existence and specifications of sciDROP-PICO solid-handling options: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Academic prototypes for acoustic ejection of microparticles / nanoparticles / dry powders | **No directly usable full-text evidence retrieved** for free dry-powder acoustic microdispensing. Search surfaced candidate acoustic/particle items, but accessible evidence remained liquid ADE or particle-in-liquid contexts; some potentially relevant items were unobtainable. | Quantitative specs for dry-powder acoustic prototypes: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Only broad acoustic-droplet literature was retrieved; no directly citable dry-powder prototype with accessible quantitative data was available. | Prototype names, particle-count dispense, powder mass per shot, dry-powder accuracy, and throughput: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Mettler Toledo **Quantos QX1 / QX2 modules** | **No module-specific QX1/QX2 datasheet evidence retrieved.** Paper-level evidence exists for the broader Quantos platform: Jiang et al. benchmarked a Quantos **XPE206 dosing system** against Chemspeed and a dual-arm robot; Christensen et al. classify Quantos as a hopper/feeder gravimetric module using rotary tapping (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 5-7, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10, christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6). | From Jiang et al.: benchmark target masses spanned **20 mg to 1 g**; example Quantos dispense times for sodium nitrite were about **30 s (20 mg), 73 s (200 mg), 72 s (500 mg), 128 s (1000 mg)**; accuracy/failure data across 13 solids are reported as percentage errors/failures in tables, including low error on some benign solids and large failures on challenging ones (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 10-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media d9cf45fa, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media ab786c08). QX1/QX2 minimum dose/range, formal stated accuracy, and head catalog: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Quantos described as **static**; receiving container can be brought by **hand, carousel, or robotic arm**. Chronect XPR is noted as integrating Quantos dispensing with a **6-axis robotic arm**; Christensen also notes integration on an Axel Semrau Chronect platform (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 2-3, christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6). | Explicit **QX1 vs QX2** specs; minimum dose; official accuracy/tolerance; throughput in doses/hour; dosing head types; explicit **XPR/XPE compatibility matrix**; APIs/software integration details: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Quantos platform behavior on powder classes | **Yes.** Christensen states hopper/feeder modules such as Quantos are best for **free-flowing** solids; Jiang notes limitations with **abrasive**, **hygroscopic/deliquescent**, and **large-granule** solids, and susceptibility of heads to damage/failure on difficult powders (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 10-11, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 7-8). | Quantitative performance is comparative, not datasheet-grade: Jiang reports platform-level percentage error/failure outcomes over multiple materials and target masses; no official vendor sub-mg spec was retrievable (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 10-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10). | Compatible with automation via Chronect-style robotic systems; manual/carousel/robot delivery to balance explicitly noted (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 2-3, christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6). | Official powder-compatibility classes by QX1/QX2 head; humidity control requirements; approved particle-size limits: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Chemspeed **Powdernium** branding | **No evidence retrieved** for the specific branding term “Powdernium.” | N/A — **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | N/A — **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Whether “Powdernium” is an official Chemspeed family/brand and its associated specs: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Chemspeed **GDU-Pfd** | **Yes, paper-level evidence.** Jiang et al. benchmark the **Chemspeed gravimetric dispensing unit for fine powder dosing (GDU-Pfd / GDU-Access Pfd)** against Quantos and a robot; they report comparative accuracy/failure results and example dispense times (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 5-7, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 7-8, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 10-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media d9cf45fa, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media ab786c08). | Benchmark target masses: **20 mg to 1 g**. Jiang reports percentage error/failure across 13 solids; e.g., Chemspeed performed well on some runs but had occasional large overshoots/failures, and Table 6 provides material-by-material error values; Table 4 gives example sodium-nitrite dispense times for comparison (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 7-8, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 10-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media d9cf45fa, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media ab786c08). Official minimum dose, datasheet accuracy at sub-mg/mg/10 mg/100 mg, and container volumes: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Jiang notes GDU-Pfd as a commercial benchmark platform; dosing cartridge/head can clog or stick with hygroscopic/deliquescent materials; dosing parameters can be manually selected or auto-tuned (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 2-3). | Official minimum dose; guaranteed accuracy classes; supported vial/container volumes; throughput benchmarks by powder class; supported cohesiveness classes from vendor datasheet: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Chemspeed **GDU-S / SWILE** | **Yes, qualitative paper evidence.** Christensen identifies **GDU-S SWILE** as a positive-displacement gravimetric module using a capillary/piston concept and states it is effective for **sub-milligram to low-milligram** dispense quantities; Jiang also describes the SWILE concept qualitatively (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 1-2). | “Sub-milligram to low-milligram” effectiveness is retrievable qualitatively, but exact minimum dose, accuracy, dispense time, and container formats are **Not retrievable in current corpus** (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 1-2). | Positive-displacement style; noted as advantageous for **sticky/oily** materials relative to hopper/feeder approaches (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6). | Exact minimum dose; formal precision/CV; speed; supported container volumes; vendor part numbers; full powder-class compatibility: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Chemspeed **GDU-V** | **No specific evidence retrieved**. | Minimum dose, accuracy, dispense time, container volume: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Integration/compatibility: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Existence/details in accessible corpus, including sub-mg/mg/10 mg/100 mg performance: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |
| Chemspeed **GDU-P** | **No specific evidence retrieved**. | Minimum dose, accuracy, dispense time, container volume: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Integration/compatibility: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. | Existence/details in accessible corpus, including sub-mg/mg/10 mg/100 mg performance: **Not retrievable in current corpus**. |


*Table: This table consolidates what was and was not retrievable from the current accessible corpus for dry-powder acoustic dispensing, Quantos, and Chemspeed GDU systems. It highlights that Jiang et al. 2023 and Christensen et al. 2021 provide useful comparative paper evidence for Quantos and Chemspeed platforms, but not the requested vendor-grade QX1/QX2 or Powdernium/GDU-family datasheet specifications.*

## (b) and (c): what is retrievable (paper-grade) for Quantos and Chemspeed powder dosing
### Comparative performance and throughput (peer-reviewed)
A peer-reviewed benchmarking study compared a **Mettler Toledo Quantos (XPE206 dosing system)** against a **Chemspeed gravimetric dispensing unit for fine powder dosing (GDU-Pfd / GDU-Access Pfd)** and a custom dual-arm robotic solid handler for 13 solids and multiple target masses (20 mg to 1 g). (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 5-7)

The paper provides *quantitative* performance evidence as percentage weighing error and failures across many materials/targets (Table 6), but this is *not* a vendor specification sheet and does not map to QX1/QX2 module parameters. (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 10-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media d9cf45fa)

The same study provides *quantitative dispense-time* benchmarks for at least one material (sodium nitrite) at multiple target masses (Table 4), with the Quantos dispensing times reported on the order of tens to ~100+ seconds depending on target mass. (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media ab786c08)

### Integration/automation evidence (qualitative)
The benchmarking paper describes Quantos-style dispensing as a **static** system where receiving containers can be delivered to the analytical balance by **hand, carousel, or robotic arm**, and notes a commercial automation configuration where a Chronect XPR integrates Quantos dispensing with a six-axis robot. (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 2-3)

A separate peer-reviewed automation perspective categorizes **Quantos** as a hopper/feeder (gravimetric) module that controls flow via **rotary tapping**, and cites **Chemspeed GDU-S SWILE** as a positive-displacement gravimetric module reported to be effective at **sub-milligram to low-milligram** dispense quantities (qualitative, not a numeric minimum dose). (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

## (a): dry-powder acoustic microdispensing (explicitly not retrievable here)
Despite targeted searches, this run did **not** retrieve citable full-text evidence that:
- EDC Biosystems **ATS Gen5/Gen6** were used for *dry powder* or *free solid particles* (as opposed to liquid compound dispensing workflows). (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)
- Labcyte/Beckman **Echo** supports or has published evaluations for *dry powder* dispensing. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)
- Scienion **sciDROP-PICO** has a documented solid/powder dispensing option with datasheet-grade specs. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)
- Academic prototypes demonstrate *free dry-powder acoustic ejection* with accessible quantitative specs in the retrieved corpus. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

## Figures/tables retrieved
Cropped images were retrieved from the benchmarking paper for (i) **Table 6** (percent dispensing error across materials/targets and platforms) and (ii) **Table 4** (dispense time comparison). These are the only retrieved visual evidence directly containing quantitative performance comparisons for Quantos vs Chemspeed in the accessible corpus. (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media d9cf45fa, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media ab786c08)

## Summary of irretrievable items (within this tool run)
The following were specifically requested but **not retrievable in current corpus**:
- (a) Any datasheet/paper quantifying dry-powder/solid-particle acoustic dispensing for EDC ATS Gen5/Gen6, Labcyte/Beckman Echo, Scienion sciDROP-PICO, or academic dry-powder acoustic ejection prototypes.
- (b) Quantos **QX1/QX2** module datasheet items: minimum dose, stated accuracy/precision, throughput specs, dosing head catalog, explicit XPR/XPE compatibility mapping, and integration/API documentation.
- (c) Chemspeed **Powdernium** branding evidence and GDU family (GDU-V, GDU-Pfd, GDU-S/SWILE, GDU-P) datasheet items: minimum dose, accuracy at sub-mg/mg/10 mg/100 mg targets, container volumes, supported powder cohesiveness classes, and dispense-time benchmarks beyond what appears in the Jiang et al. benchmarking tables.

In the retrieved materials, Quantos and Chemspeed details are available mainly as *comparative experimental performance* (errors/failures, limited dispense-time examples) and *qualitative architecture/integration descriptions*, not as module-level datasheet specifications. (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 2-3, christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 5-7)

- Academic prototypes demonstrate *free dry-powder acoustic ejection* with accessible quantitative specs in the retrieved corpus. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

## Figures/tables retrieved
Cropped images were retrieved from the benchmarking paper for (i) **Table 6** (percent dispensing error across materials/targets and platforms) and (ii) **Table 4** (dispense time comparison). These are the only retrieved visual evidence directly containing quantitative performance comparisons for Quantos vs Chemspeed in the accessible corpus. (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media d9cf45fa, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid media ab786c08)

## Summary of irretrievable items (within this tool run)
The following were specifically requested but **not retrievable in current corpus**:
- (a) Any datasheet/paper quantifying dry-powder/solid-particle acoustic dispensing for EDC ATS Gen5/Gen6, Labcyte/Beckman Echo, Scienion sciDROP-PICO, or academic dry-powder acoustic ejection prototypes.
- (b) Quantos **QX1/QX2** module datasheet items: minimum dose, stated accuracy/precision, throughput specs, dosing head catalog, explicit XPR/XPE compatibility mapping, and integration/API documentation.
- (c) Chemspeed **Powdernium** branding evidence and GDU family (GDU-V, GDU-Pfd, GDU-S/SWILE, GDU-P) datasheet items: minimum dose, accuracy at sub-mg/mg/10 mg/100 mg targets, container volumes, supported powder cohesiveness classes, and dispense-time benchmarks beyond what appears in the Jiang et al. benchmarking tables.

In the retrieved materials, Quantos and Chemspeed details are available mainly as *comparative experimental performance* (errors/failures, limited dispense-time examples) and *qualitative architecture/integration descriptions*, not as module-level datasheet specifications. (jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 8-10, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 2-3, christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 5-7)
